title: HuggingFace Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**HuggingFace** is the ML-hub provider exposing three REST surfaces under separate base URLs. This connector ŌĆö `HuggingFaceConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "huggingface"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) ŌĆö wraps every surface a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base URL | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Hub API | `https://huggingface.co/api` | Models, datasets, spaces, organizations, whoami |
| Inference API | `https://api-inference.huggingface.co` | Serverless text-generation, embeddings, classification, summarization, translation, image classification |
| Inference Endpoints | `https://api.endpoints.huggingface.cloud/v2` | Managed dedicated model deployments (list/get/create/delete/invoke) |

The connector normalises **models** into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 503 model-loading + 429 rate-limit + 5xx with exponential / hinted backoff, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC ŌĆö all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::HuggingFaceHTTPClient`).

**Hub vs Inference vs Endpoints ŌĆö read this once and the rest of the doc makes sense.**

- **Hub** is the catalogue: it tells you what models, datasets, and spaces exist, who owns them, how many downloads they have, what tasks they support. No GPUs involved.
- **Inference API** is the serverless invocation surface: you POST `{inputs: ...}` to `https://api-inference.huggingface.co/models/{model}` and HuggingFace runs the model on a shared GPU pool. The first call to a cold model returns HTTP 503 with `estimated_time` ŌĆö the client honours this and sleeps before retrying.
- **Inference Endpoints** is the managed-deployment surface: you reserve a dedicated GPU and HuggingFace returns a served URL (your own subdomain). No cold starts, predictable latency, billed per-hour.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a HuggingFace API token

1. Sign in to https://huggingface.co.
2. Open **Settings ŌåÆ Access Tokens** (https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens).
3. Click **New token**, set:
   - **Name** ŌĆö `shielva-connector` (or whatever you prefer).
   - **Role** ŌĆö `read` for browsing + serverless inference; `write` if you need to manage Inference Endpoints.
4. Click **Generate a token**, copy the value ŌĆö it begins with `hf_ŌĆ”`. HuggingFace shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ŌåÆ Manage Connectors ŌåÆ HuggingFace**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | The token from step 4 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://huggingface.co/api` only for a private mirror |
| `inference_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api-inference.huggingface.co` for proxies |
| `endpoints_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.endpoints.huggingface.cloud/v2` for self-hosted gateways |
| `default_model` (text, optional) | e.g. `meta-llama/Llama-3-8B-Instruct` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `HuggingFaceHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which hits `GET /whoami-v2` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx ŌåÆ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 ŌåÆ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 ŌåÆ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
HuggingFace REST API uses **server-to-server API token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <api_key>` (standard Bearer prefix ŌĆö unlike Wix which sends the raw key).
- The same token works on all three surfaces (Hub + Inference + Inference Endpoints) ŌĆö no separate keys.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Initialises `HuggingFaceHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any HuggingFace endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` with the api_key as `access_token` and `token_type="Bearer"`. No exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /whoami-v2` ŌĆö minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called ŌĆö no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None ŌĆö the api_key lives in `self.config`. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Content-Type:  application/json          ŌåÉ JSON bodies
Content-Type:  application/octet-stream  ŌåÉ binary image/audio bodies
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ŌĆö never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults `https://huggingface.co/api`) | Hub override |
| `inference_url` | text | no | `self.inference_url` (defaults `https://api-inference.huggingface.co`) | Inference API override |
| `endpoints_url` | text | no | `self.endpoints_url` (defaults `https://api.endpoints.huggingface.cloud/v2`) | Managed Endpoints override |
| `default_model` | text | no | `self.default_model` | Default inference model id |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Back-compat

Older installs used the key `api_token` ŌĆö the connector still reads it as a fallback for `api_key` so existing tenants keep working without re-entering credentials.

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_HUB_BASE       = "https://huggingface.co/api"
_INFERENCE_BASE = "https://api-inference.huggingface.co"
_ENDPOINTS_BASE = "https://api.endpoints.huggingface.cloud/v2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `HuggingFaceConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::HuggingFaceHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; initialise `HuggingFaceHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth ŌĆö returns a TokenInfo whose access_token is the api_key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Hits `GET /whoami-v2` as a lightweight probe. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Whoamis, lists up to 100 models by the authenticated author, normalises each ŌåÆ `NormalizedDocument` ŌåÆ `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Hub (Models / Datasets / Spaces / Orgs)
children.content:
### `async whoami() -> Dict`
`GET /whoami-v2` ŌĆö authenticated user's identity payload.

### `async list_models(*, search?, author?, filter?, limit=20, sort="downloads") -> List[Dict]`
`GET /models?limit=&sort=&search=&author=&filter=` ŌĆö common filters: `filter="text-generation"`, `sort="downloads"`.

### `async get_model(model_id) -> Dict`
`GET /models/{model_id}`. Accepts slash-bearing ids (`meta-llama/Llama-3-8B-Instruct`) as raw path segments.

### `async list_datasets(*, search?, limit=20) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /datasets?limit=&search=`.

### `async get_dataset(dataset_id) -> Dict`
`GET /datasets/{dataset_id}`.

### `async list_spaces(*, search?, limit=20) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /spaces?limit=&search=`.

### `async list_organization_repos(organization) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{name}` ŌĆö public repos of an org.

children.title: Inference API (Serverless)
children.content:
All inference methods target `POST {inference_url}/models/{model}`. The body shape is `{inputs, parameters?}` for JSON, raw bytes for image/audio.

### `async run_inference(model, inputs, parameters?) -> Any`
Generic JSON entrypoint ŌĆö use for any task not covered by a named helper.

### `async text_generation(model, inputs, parameters?) -> Any`
Returns `[{generated_text: "..."}]` for text-generation models.

### `async feature_extraction(model, inputs) -> Any`
Returns embedding vector(s) for sentence-transformer models.

### `async text_classification(model, inputs) -> Any`
Returns `[[{label, score}, ...]]` for classifier models.

### `async summarization(model, inputs, parameters?) -> Any`
Returns `[{summary_text: "..."}]`.

### `async translation(model, inputs) -> Any`
Returns `[{translation_text: "..."}]`.

### `async image_classification(model, image_bytes) -> Any`
POSTs raw bytes with `Content-Type: application/octet-stream`. Returns `[{label, score}, ...]`.

children.title: Inference Endpoints (Managed Deployments)
children.content:
### `async list_endpoints() -> Dict`
`GET {endpoints_url}/endpoint` ŌĆö list all managed deployments for the account.

### `async get_endpoint(endpoint_name) -> Dict`
`GET {endpoints_url}/endpoint/{name}` ŌĆö read a single deployment's spec + status.

### `async create_endpoint(payload) -> Dict`
`POST {endpoints_url}/endpoint` ŌĆö create a managed deployment. `payload` includes name, type (`protected`/`public`), provider (`vendor`, `region`), compute (`accelerator`, `instanceType`), and model (`repository`).

### `async delete_endpoint(endpoint_name) -> Dict`
`DELETE {endpoints_url}/endpoint/{name}`.

### `async run_inference_endpoint(endpoint_url, payload) -> Any`
`POST {endpoint_url}` ŌĆö invoke a managed endpoint by the served URL HuggingFace returns (e.g. `https://<endpoint>.huggingface.cloud/predict`). Bypasses cold-start because the GPU is dedicated.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
HuggingFaceError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ HuggingFaceAuthError                  # 401 / 403
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ HuggingFaceBadRequestError            # 400
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ HuggingFaceNotFound                   # 404
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ HuggingFaceRateLimitError             # 429 ŌĆö retry_after_s
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ HuggingFaceModelLoadingError          # 503 cold-load ŌĆö estimated_time
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ HuggingFaceServerError                # 5xx
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ HuggingFaceNetworkError               # transport-level (timeout, DNS, reset)
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
HuggingFaceAPIError      = HuggingFaceServerError    # legacy name from older code
HuggingFaceNotFoundError = HuggingFaceNotFound
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `1s ┬Ę 2 ** attempt + jitter` (cap 32s), retry up to `max_retries=3` |
| 503 (model loading) | Sleep `estimated_time` seconds (cap 32s), then retry ŌĆö honours the HF hint |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff as 429, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `HuggingFaceNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
NetworkError ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Generate at huggingface.co/settings/tokens, paste into ACP. HuggingFace shows the token only once ŌĆö rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401/403 from HuggingFace ŌĆö token revoked or lacks scope | In Settings ŌåÆ Access Tokens, regenerate with `read` (Hub + serverless inference) or `write` (Endpoints management) scope. |
| `huggingface.model_loading.retry` warnings spike | Cold-start on first call to a model | Normal for the serverless Inference API. The client sleeps for `estimated_time` then retries. To eliminate cold-starts, provision an Inference Endpoint via `create_endpoint(...)` and call `run_inference_endpoint(served_url, payload)` instead. |
| `huggingface.rate_limit.retry` warnings spike | Hitting the HuggingFace free-tier rate cap | Lower `rate_limit_per_min`, batch fewer concurrent requests, or use a Pro account / Inference Endpoint. |
| `HuggingFaceModelLoadingError` after 3 retries | Model is huge (>30GB) and exceeds the 32s cold-start cap | Provision an Inference Endpoint for warm reservation, or call once to start the warm-up and retry from the caller after a longer wait. |
| 404 on `get_model("foo")` | Wrong id (missing org prefix) | Use `owner/model` for org-owned repos (e.g. `meta-llama/Llama-3-8B-Instruct`). |
| 404 on `get_dataset("foo")` | Gated/private dataset | Make sure the token's user has access (accept the dataset card on the website first). |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'HuggingFaceAPIError'` | Older code path | Already covered ŌĆö `HuggingFaceAPIError = HuggingFaceServerError` alias preserved in `exceptions.py`. |
| Endpoint creation 400 with `Invalid compute spec` | Compute requested isn't available in the chosen region | Use `list_endpoints()` to see existing deployments for valid compute combinations or consult HuggingFace's Endpoints catalogue. |
